description_long: This is a vertical, rectangular red lever arch file with a sturdy, textured exterior and reinforced metal corners. The front face features a large white label with blue accents and a circular finger hole for easy retrieval from shelves. The side view reveals two black recessed finger grips, while the top view shows the open, hollow interior with two large metal rings for securing documents. The back and side panels are solid and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The object is constructed primarily from rigid cardboard or fiberboard, covered in a red laminate, with metal reinforcements and mechanisms. This type of file is commonly used for organizing and storing documents in offices or archives.
description: A red lever arch file with a white label, metal reinforcements, and a circular finger hole, designed for document storage.
description_view_0: Front view shows the white label, blue branding, and circular finger hole.
description_view_1: Side view displays the solid red panel and two black recessed finger grips.
description_view_2: Top view reveals the open interior with two metal rings and the finger hole.
description_view_3: Back view shows a plain red surface with metal corner reinforcements.
